target_audience: A framework operator deciding whether a human-facing weak-artifact rejection remains visible on the runtime reopen path instead of collapsing into a generic disagreement record.
expected_runtime_effect: A weak human-facing artifact family should stay attached to the reopen path rather than disappearing behind a generic human override label.
The runtime record should preserve both the weak-artifact critique and the reopened execution chain.
The resulting verdict should make human-facing quality rejection inspectable as a runtime family, not only as a fixture policy.
remaining_gap: The current runtime family still reuses one live verification path rather than multiple independent disconfirmation sources.
Additional runtime-generated weak-artifact reopen families should vary artifact type and rejection source before the v3.1.0 gate closes.
